title: The Water Cycle
content:
Water is always moving around our Earth. It goes on a great journey called the water cycle.

Let's follow a drop of water named Drip on her adventure!

Drip lived in the ocean. One sunny day, she got very warm. She felt herself getting lighter and lighter.

"I'm floating up!" Drip said. She had turned into water vapor and was rising into the sky.

This is called evaporation. The sun's heat turns water into an invisible gas.

Up, up, up Drip floated until she reached the clouds. It was cold up there! She turned back into a tiny water drop.

This is called condensation. When water vapor cools, it turns back into water.

Drip joined millions of other water drops in the cloud. The cloud got bigger and heavier.

Soon it was too heavy to stay up in the sky.

"Here we go!" said Drip as she fell back to Earth as rain.

This is called precipitation. Rain, snow, and hail are all types of precipitation.

Drip landed in a stream. The stream flowed into a river. The river flowed back to the ocean.

And there, Drip waited for another sunny day to start her journey all over again.

The water cycle never stops. The same water has been going around and around for millions of years!

The End.

author: Educational
